title: Classification of Signals
summary: This page introduces the classification of signals into five categories: continuous-time vs. discrete-time, analog vs. digital, periodic vs. aperiodic, energy vs. power, and deterministic vs. probabilistic. It clarifies the distinction between continuous-time/discrete-time (relating to the time axis) and analog/digital (relating to amplitude values), noting these concepts are often confused. An analog signal can take any value in a continuous range, while a digital signal has a finite number of amplitude values.
